text: A 5x6 chessboard is missing its two opposite corner squares. Can the remaining 28 squares be tiled by 1x2 dominoes? Answer yes or no and explain why.
A jar contains 10 red and 15 blue marbles. Each move, you remove two marbles of different colors and put back one red marble. What color is the last marble remaining?
Starting with the number 1, you may either double the current number or add 11. Can you reach exactly 200? Answer yes or no.
A 3x3 grid has 9 cells each containing the number 1. You may pick any 2x2 subgrid and multiply every number in it by -1. Can you make all 9 cells contain -1? Answer yes or no.
A sequence starts with 1, 2, 3, ..., 2023. In each step you replace any two numbers a and b with the single number a*b - a - b + 2. What is the final number when only one number remains?
You have 2024 coins all showing heads. Each move flips exactly 7 coins. Can you reach a state where all coins show tails? Answer yes or no.
A 4x4 chessboard is tiled by 1x2 dominoes. Each domino is colored either red or blue. Prove or disprove that the number of dominoes whose long side is horizontal must be even.
On a number line, a frog starts at 0. Each jump moves it exactly 3 units left or right. Can it ever land on 1? Answer yes or no.
A bag has 5 white and 6 black balls. Each operation removes two balls; if they match, replace with one white ball; if they differ, replace with one black ball. What color is the last ball?
There are 100 switches in a row, all off. Each move flips three consecutive switches. Can you turn exactly one switch on and leave all others off? Answer yes or no.
A positive integer n is written on a board. You may replace n with either n - 7 or 2n. Starting from 100, can you reach 50? Answer yes or no.
An 8x8 chessboard has its top-left and bottom-right corner squares removed. Can the remaining 62 squares be covered by 31 dominoes of size 1x2? Answer yes or no.
You write the integers 1 through 30 on a blackboard. At each step you erase two numbers and write their absolute difference. Is the final remaining number odd or even?
In a room, 15 people each shake hands with an odd number of others in the room. Is the total number of handshakes odd or even?
Starting from (0, 0), each move adds one of (1,2), (2,1), (1,-2), or (-2,1) to the current position. Can you reach (0, 1)? Answer yes or no.
A 7x7 board has three corner squares removed leaving 46 squares. Can these 46 squares be tiled by 1x2 dominoes? Answer yes or no.
You have 99 odd numbers written on a board. You repeatedly replace any two numbers a and b with a + b - 1. What is the parity of the final number?
A sequence of H's and T's starts as HHHH. You may reverse any contiguous block of at least 2 characters. Can you reach TTTT? Answer yes or no.
There are 20 coins in a circle, alternating heads and tails. Each move flips two adjacent coins simultaneously. Can you make all coins show heads? Answer yes or no.
The number 7 is written on a board. Each step you may multiply by 3 or add 5. Can you reach exactly 100? Answer yes or no.

Start with the multiset {1, 2, 3, ..., 10}. Each operation picks two numbers x and y and replaces them with (x+y)/2 and (x+y)/2. Can all 10 numbers become integers after some sequence of operations? Answer yes or no.
A frog sits at position 0 on the integers. Each jump is either +5 or -3. Can the frog reach position 1 after exactly 6 jumps? Answer yes or no.
A 2x2023 rectangle is to be tiled with 1x2 and 2x1 dominoes. Is the number of tilings odd or even?

Seven integers are arranged in a circle. At each step, you replace each number with the difference of its two neighbors. After one full step applied simultaneously, what is the parity relationship between the new sum and the old sum?
You have a 1x10 strip of squares. A token starts on square 1. Each move shifts the token 2, 3, or 5 squares to the right. Can the token land exactly on square 10? Answer yes or no.
In a game, you start with a pile of 48 stones. A move consists of splitting any pile into two nonempty piles. You want all piles to have size 3. Is this possible? Answer yes or no.
The integers 1 through 40 are written on a board. Each move erases two numbers and writes their sum minus 1. What is the final number?
A knight starts at corner a1 of a standard 8x8 chessboard. After exactly 64 moves can the knight return to a1, visiting every other square exactly once? Answer yes or no.
Each of 12 students shakes hands with each of the others exactly once. Is the total number of handshakes divisible by 4?
A 6x6 board has all four corners removed, leaving 32 squares. Can these 32 squares be tiled by 1x2 dominoes? Answer yes or no.
